blocks.content: They stole the Tagis?
Mitgun, who was always confident in front of the leader, couldn't help but bow his head this time.
Yameng's elite troops had been defeated.
There were no signs of a struggle at the battle site he inspected. It was a one-sided beating.
More than half of the bodies showed signs of being killed instantly.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Yes, that's what happened. We're currently searching for them.
Bang!
Frankwine's punch crumpled the iron table inward.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: You don't know who did it? There must be an organization familiar with the secret routes among those who attacked Yameng!

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: But there's no organization in the homeland capable of taking down my subordinates so easily. You know that.
Frankwine's punch struck Mitgun.
With a loud crack, his tall body slammed into the wall.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: But they were taken down! Who was it? If you don't know, you'd better make something up.
Mitgun got up, wiping the blood from his lips.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: If he didn't pull himself together quickly, he might die.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: If I had to guess, it would be them. The ones who have been catching Rank S prey every day.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Armin, or whatever his name is?

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: If they had to name suspects, it was definitely them.
Each of them was highly skilled, and the ability to kill his subordinates was rare even in the homeland.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: But why? Wasn't the Tagis being transported to the rebel command?

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Hmm, I guess I need to check it out.
Frankwine put on his coat and said, We're going to the First Command.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: How many subordinates should we gather?

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Subordinates? Do you think they can handle this? Just you and me.
Mitgun's eyes changed.
It was a signal between the two who had crossed countless deadly situations to settle in Purgatory.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Understood.
Mitgun gritted his teeth and stretched his neck.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: He didn't mind being hit by Frankwine for no reason, but he was itching to deal with the scum who had backstabbed them.
The atmosphere at the First Rebel Command was the tensest it had been since Babel's invasion.
Commander Crude, accompanied by Gaold's group, greeted Frankwine.
The rebel soldiers stood fully armed, but Frankwine walked in as if he owned the place.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Hmm, do you live in a place like this? It's more rundown than I thought.
It was understandable, given that half of the facilities had been destroyed by Babel's attack.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: What happened? The Tagis hasn't arrived even though the scheduled time has passed.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Why are you asking me? We sent it.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Are you serious right now…?
As Crude stepped forward with a glare, Sein placed a hand on his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Wait. I'll handle this.
Sein looked Frankwine over with a cold gaze.
The leader of Yameng.
He had thought of him as just a merchant from the homeland, but the aura he exuded was extraordinary.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: You said you sent the Tagis a while ago?

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Yes, we did.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: But it hasn't arrived here?

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Well, that's your problem, not mine.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: The goods were sent but didn't arrive? Hijacked?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Are you suspecting us?

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Suspecting?
A violent glint flashed in Frankwine's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Are you joking? Do you all want to die?
Ugh!
As if the air had turned toxic, Crude's lungs ached, and he stepped back, holding his breath.
The rebels aimed their arcs but couldn't bring themselves to shoot. The vague thought that they would die the moment they pulled the trigger filled their minds.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: You'd better confess while I'm being nice. This is clearly your doing. Who was it?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: They've finally moved.
Yameng's strength was said to be the best even in the homeland.
If they had lost subordinates and come all the way here, the hijackers must be highly skilled.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Let's go somewhere quiet.
In the command and control room, Sein sat Frankwine down and said, First, I'd like to hear what happened.
Mitgun tilted his head.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: If a massive amount of minerals and elixirs used to make the Tagis had disappeared, it would be natural to rage and try to pin the blame on someone.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: But the man in front of him was more interested in the hijacking process than the goods.
When Frankwine crossed his arms and signaled, Mitgun began to explain.
Of course, he could only describe the state of the bodies after everything had ended.
But it was enough information for Sein.
About ten bodies were cut by something sharp. One body was completely exploded into mush.
And that one body was the leader.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Roche of Metal, Tarvan of Vibration. Cage B Team.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Though not as synchronized as Cage A Team, Roche and Tarvan had been comrades since childhood and were the most powerful dealers in Cage B Team.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: You seem to know something. If so, we have a grievance too. As for the goods…

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: The Tagis doesn't matter.
Frankwine frowned at Gaold's words.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Doesn't matter? What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I mean we won't hold you responsible for it.
Gaold took Sein's place and sat down, and Frankwine's gaze swept over him from head to toe.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: This guy is on a different level of power.
Everyone here was skilled, but Gaold had a different nature.
Battle-hardened? No, he had fought battles beyond that.
Just like himself.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Not holding us responsible means…

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Instead, we have a request. There's something you need to find for us. If you're the largest community in the homeland, you can do it.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Find what?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It was a short word, but it was enough to make Frankwine frown deeply.
Frankwine's shoulders shook.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Does this guy think I'm a pushover?
Bang!
Frankwine kicked the table and swung his fist at Gaold.
Of course, Gaold didn't just take the hit.
Shirone's group was startled by the sudden fight, but Mitgun charged in with his fists raised as if he had been waiting for this.
Etella blocked him, and their rapid punches were exchanged.
As Etella's fist aimed for his side, Mitgun blocked it with his elbow.
Boom!
The vibration of the Yin-Yang Wave Fist spread through Mitgun's body like ripples.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: A wave?
Mitgun was pushed sideways and collided with the wall.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: But Etella couldn't follow up. The same kind of shock was spreading through his own body.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: What is this?

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Annoying…
As Gaold prepared to cast a spell with an irritated expression, Etella shouted.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: No! That person is…
A powerful air press crushed the command and control room.
All the equipment on the ground was flattened, and Frankwine and Mitgun's backs bent under the pressure.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Mirror Cycle.
The next moment, all the pressure in the space was directed at Gaold.
Boom!
The spot where he stood caved in, and Gaold felt his own power for the first time.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ha ha, I still have a long way to go.
Gaold grinned, and silence fell over the command and control room.
Frankwine and Mitgun were shocked by Gaold's unscathed state, and the others were surprised to see Gaold affected even slightly.
Sein turned his gaze to Mitgun.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What a strange ability. It doesn't reflect the shock but seems to repel the event itself.
The first thing that came to mind was an irregular type, but even his Iron Wheel Eye couldn't detect any special activation conditions.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: The only guess was that they were from another world.

blocks.speaker: Mitgun
blocks.content: Phew, this is…
As Mitgun rubbed his fists and walked out, a crack appeared on his face.
Frankwine, watching Mitgun touch his cheek in disbelief, turned his gaze back to Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: The Mirror Cycle broke? This guy is incredibly dangerous.
In the original world, there weren't many who could match Mitgun.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: As he realized this, his cumbersome anger subsided, and his merchant's mind began to work again.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Fine, I'll find it. But we have conditions too.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Give us the facilities of the Second Rebel Command, Raysis's facilities. They're useless to you now anyway.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Fine. As long as you find it properly.
Frankwine snorted and turned away.
Crack. Crack.
As Mitgun moved his facial muscles, more cracks appeared on his face, but they soon healed.
That night.
As the group gathered, Gaold spoke.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'll be away for a while. Prepare to attack as soon as the Tagis support arrives.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Where are you going? If it's a mission, take the team…

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'll be back by tomorrow. Keep the house safe, you mutts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is he going to find the Tagis?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Probably. If what Hel said is true, Miro's life is hanging by a thread. He knows there's no time to delay.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But how does he know where to find it?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Gaold doesn't need to find it. They'll come to him.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: You said his pain threshold increased to a million times?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Yes, that's the level of power I witnessed.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: That's dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: I agree. If even a little…

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: No, that's not what I mean. When I say it's dangerous, I mean his mental state. Since coming to Heaven, Gaold's pain threshold has risen, and his power has increased significantly. And now he knows Miro's location. Even if he doesn't show it, he's probably not in his right mind.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Should we track him now?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Impossible. As annoying as he is, there's no way to follow him. We have to leave it to him for now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I've been curious for a while, what happened between the Chairman and Miro?
It was an innocent question, but everyone perked up their ears.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: This isn't the place to talk about it. Let's move somewhere else.
